title: vLLM 0.11.2 MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor API Removal on Ascend NPU
symptom: When serving multimodal Vision-Language Models (VLMs) with M-RoPE position encoding (e.g., Qwen2-VL, Qwen2.5-VL, MinerU2.5-Pro) on Ascend NPU with vLLM 0.11.2+, vllm_ascend plugin crashes at runtime with AttributeError: `type object 'MRotaryEmbedding' has no attribute 'get_input_positions_tensor'`. The error occurs in `NPUModelRunner._init_mrope_positions()` when initializing M-RoPE position embeddings for multimodal requests.
root_cause: vLLM 0.11.2 restructured the M-RoPE position computation API. The static method `MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor(input_tokens_tensor)` was removed and replaced with an instance method `model.get_mrope_input_positions(prompt_token_ids, mm_features)` that directly consumes multimodal features. The vllm_ascend plugin's `NPUModelRunner._init_mrope_positions()` was adapted from an older version of `gpu_model_runner.py` and still uses the deprecated static method. This is a vLLM version-specific breaking change — any vllm_ascend deployment that upgrades to vLLM 0.11.2+ with M-RoPE models will encounter this.
fix_steps: 2. Replace the old static method call: `MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor(req_state.mrope_positions_data)` → new instance method call: `model.get_mrope_input_positions(req_state.prompt_token_ids, req_state.mm_features)`. Reference: upstream `gpu_model_runner.py:955-964`.
3. Add `supports_mrope` import from `vllm.model_executor.models.interfaces` at the top of model_runner_v1.py. This import is required for the mrope support assertion.
4. Add an assertion guard: `assert supports_mrope(model), 'M-RoPE support is not implemented.'` before calling `model.get_mrope_input_positions()`. This matches the upstream pattern.
5. Remove the obsolete mm_feature field extraction loop — the new `get_mrope_input_positions()` takes `req_state.mm_features` directly, handling feature extraction internally.

6. Do NOT remove `MRotaryEmbedding` import — the class is still used elsewhere (`get_next_input_positions_tensor()` for decode-phase token-by-token position updates). Only the static method was removed.
7. Run `lsp_diagnostics` on model_runner_v1.py to verify no import errors or type mismatches.
8. Re-launch vLLM serving and verify M-RoPE models initialize successfully without the AttributeError.
affected_patterns: Static method `MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor()` — removed in vLLM 0.11.2, replaced by instance method `model.get_mrope_input_positions()`
Multimodal feature extraction in `_init_mrope_positions()` — old code manually extracted mm_features from req_state; new API consumes them directly
Missing `supports_mrope` import — only needed in vLLM 0.11.2+ where the function was moved to `vllm.model_executor.models.interfaces`
Any M-RoPE position initialization code in Ascend model runner that predates vLLM 0.11.2

code_changes.description: _init_mrope_positions: replaced static MRotaryEmbedding.get_input_positions_tensor() with instance model.get_mrope_input_positions(), added supports_mrope assertion guard
Imports: retains MRotaryEmbedding for get_next_input_positions_tensor() (decode phase), adds supports_mrope from vllm.model_executor.models.interfaces

references: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py:955-964 — upstream correct pattern: model.get_mrope_input_positions(prompt_token_ids, mm_features) with supports_mrope assertion
vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py:776-785 — post-fix implementation, confirmed to match upstream pattern exactly
vllm_ascend/worker/model_runner_v1.py:944 — MRotaryEmbedding.get_next_input_positions_tensor() still used for decode phase, confirming import retention is correct
antipatterns: Do NOT blindly add a compatibility shim for get_input_positions_tensor — the API was intentionally removed and vllm_ascend should match the new API shape.
Do NOT delete the MRotaryEmbedding import entirely — get_next_input_positions_tensor() is still used elsewhere (line 944) and deleting it would cause a new import error.
Do NOT mutate the model object inside _init_mrope_positions() — get_mrope_input_positions() returns the computed positions/deltas; assign them directly to req_state.
Do NOT skip the supports_mrope assertion — it catches configuration errors where M-RoPE models are served with a non-mrope-capable runner.

meta.refiner_reasoning: The fix is verified correct: model_runner_v1.py:776-785 now matches gpu_model_runner.py:955-964 exactly. The candidate's recommended_type 'skill' is correct — this is a vLLM version-specific API migration applicable to any M-RoPE model on Ascend. The candidate's category was corrected from 'operator_incompat' to 'code_adaptation' — the issue is an API renaming, not an operator compatibility problem. The candidate's classification reasoning is not contaminated (unique to this candidate). Confidence kept at 0.85 — the fix pattern is correct and verified, though the specific API changes may differ in future vLLM versions.
